hook: ChatGPT keeps explaining things to you like you've never heard of the topic before. Here's why.
caption:
ChatGPT keeps explaining things to you like you've never heard of the topic before. Here's why.

AI has no idea who you are.

Every single conversation, it's meeting you for the first time. It doesn't know you spent 6 years in finance before switching to design. It doesn't know you hate analogies. It doesn't know you want the answer first and the explanation only if you ask.

So it defaults to the middle.

The imaginary average user. College-educated. Probably not an expert. Probably needs a little hand-holding.

That's not you. But the AI doesn't know that.

This is why smart people feel weirdly patronized by AI. Not because the tool is bad — because it's calibrated for someone else.

The fix isn't prompting harder. It's giving AI a baseline understanding of how you actually think — your background, your communication style, what 'a good answer' looks like to you specifically.

When the AI knows you think in systems, hate fluff, and already know the basics? The responses change completely.

Same question. Completely different answer.
